title: Solenandra humilis
intro: Solenandra humilis, commonly known as the Mexican prickly poppy or Mexican prickly poppy root, is a plant species in the Bignoniaceae family. The root of Solenandra humilis has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is reported to have several health benefits, including reducing pain and swelling, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Solenandra humilis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Solenandra humilis is classified in the plant kingdom, belonging to the family Gesneriaceae. This family comprises a diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as African violets and gesneriads. Solenandra humilis is specifically placed in the genus Solenandra, which is a genus of flowering plants endemic to Mexico and Central America.
